76111.subject: A Fully Digital Wall Street
76111.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

Happy Friday! Today we look at DTCC's first live production trades of tokenized securities, run through the depository holding $114T and joined by JPMorgan, BlackRock, Goldman, and 30 other firms. Yesterday's tape was quiet, but on the one week view the Privacy Index led everything as ZEC and DCR ripped, and a soft June CPI print trimmed September hike odds.

вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

Yesterday we saw a quiet, mixed session. Oracle (+2.3%), Perps (+2.1%), and Modular (+1.8%) led while Lending (-3.0%), Ethereum Eco (-2.9%), and AI (-1.8%) lagged; BTC slipped -0.4% and the S&P 500 added +0.2%. The macro tape did the talking this week: June's consumer price index (CPI) print fell 0.4% on the month, dropping annual inflation to 3.5% against 3.8% expected, while September Fed hike odds [eased](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/consumer-price-index-inflation-report-june-2026.html) to 63% from above 75%.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/aa75817f-c381-4ffb-8433-b5608e46e617/image.png?t=1784274059)
Caption: 

The weekly view is where the story is. Privacy Index (+15.5%) led everything, ahead of Oracle (+12.0%) and Revenue Leaders (+10.2%), with BTC up +4.1%. Crypto Miners sat at the bottom at -8.1% as the AI infrastructure trade cooled; Bloomberg's [report](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-01/meta-is-building-a-cloud-business-to-sell-excess-ai-compute) that Meta plans a new unit to sell excess GPU capacity challenged the compute scarcity assumption underpinning miners' AI pivots.В 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ef58da1b-e0eb-4fc2-8389-e0f9702cf2c7/image.png?t=1784274059)
Caption: 

Within the Privacy Index, DCR (+24.9%) and ZEC (+22.7%) drove most of the gains, while ZAMA (+4.2%) and XMR (+3.2%) posted more modest increases and RAIL (-14.9%) lagged sharply. DCR broke out on July 13, jumping over 30% in 24 hours. With over two thirds of supply staked, whale-sized orders chasing the privacy rotation likely hit a thin float and forced a breakout from a multi-month descending channel. ZEC's bid is more fundamental: the [Ironwood upgrade](https://x.com/BSCNews/status/2075478125102715072?s=20), locked for July 28 activation, uses formal verification to prove there are no undetectable counterfeiting bugs after June's Orchard pool flaw disclosure.В 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-

76107.subject: Markets tumble on Iran fallout
76107.body:
TodayвҖҷs newsletter is brought to you by **[Choose Rich Live](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**[!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)

Our very own Nick OвҖҷNeill is has launched a new stream covering crypto, finance, AI, tech, and the stories shaping the internet economy.

If you already follow Nick, you know what to expect: sharp takes, market chaos, founder brain, and the occasional banger youвҖҷll see clipped on the timeline five minutes later.

Episodes will be streaming live on YouTube, so make sure youвҖҷre subscribed to catch the next one as it happens.

**[Subscribe to Choose Rich Live!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**

вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”

## Top Stories

**BTC $62,863 (-1.9%) | ETH $1,830 (-2.8%) | SOL $74 (-2.0%)**

* Nasdaq futures [tumble 2%](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2078038529418883182?s=20) as overall market reacts to continued Iran war

* Micron sinks [30%](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2077735422633930913) from June high as memory stocks slide

* Polygon Labs is [laying off staff](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2077759966786777423) and shifting from blockchain foundation to payments company

* Visa launches a new platform to offer stablecoin services to over [200 million merchants](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2077769934151847971)

* a16z linked wallet [sells $6.5M](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2078018858817098215?s=20) worth of HYPE as it falls [below $60](https://x.com/DegenerateNews/status/2077935866614268401?s=20)

* Clarity Act does [not have Democrat support](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2077882147931615657?s=20) as it is currently written

* Judge orders 25 crypto [account freezes](https://x.com/solidintel_x/status/2078047617095729507?s=20) tied to LIBRA memecoin

* Citadel [invests $400M](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2026/07/16/citadel-securities-invests-usd400-million-in-crypto-com-valuing-exchange-at-usd20-billion) in Crypto.com

* Toly says Nikita Biers вҖң[reign of terror](https://x.com/toly/status/2077520057345908816)вҖқ as a Solana advisor is over

* HYPE, Hyperliquid's native token, [drops below $60](https://x.com/DegenerateNews/status/2077935866614268401), per @DegenerateNews.

* Truth Social moves to [sell faster access](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2077843063196520633) to President Trump's posts to traders and investors, per @WatcherGuru citing the WSJ.

* The White House says China carried out the largest [election data compromise](https://x.com/tradfi/status/2077922567105736882) in history, spanning years since the 2020 election cycle

* [Iran](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2077536243718541512) releases US citizen, Trump calls it a goodwill gesture.

* US 30-year mortgage rate climbs to [6.55%](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2077791507336081893).

* French 30-year bond yield hits its [highest](https://x.com/Barchart/status/2077832103207514617) since the financial cris

Fear and Greed is at
31
(Fear)
and BTC is at
$63K
this morning. Bitcoin dipped slightly overnight after failing to
hold above $64,500 yesterday, retracing toward support as trading
volume thinned into the weekend.

The week is closing on a mix of institutional moves and
political decisions. A trillion-dollar asset manager just
launched a new kind of crypto ETF. The entire Senate aligned on a
single crypto vote. And a hardware wallet maker just drew a clear
line between what AI can and cannot do with your funds.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

T. Rowe Price Just Launched a Multi-Token Crypto ETF

T. Rowe Price entered the crypto ETF market on July 16 with
TKNZ, an actively managed fund holding Bitcoin, Ethereum, BNB,
Solana, XRP, and HYPE.

Unlike existing single-token spot ETFs, portfolio managers can
shift allocations based on market conditions and research. The
fund launched with Bitcoin as its largest holding at just over
40%.

What is HYPE's weighting in the TKNZ fund at launch?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/lQiHD7FhxUFTi1AMPcQOAg~~/AAQRxRA~/hZYtRtppAgXy66s-N3-MZuOuLH67lvQBEKiFA65gBrovEP8JX0OyH_sZV8r9GCBi0n0yMgna1hDokR69ezN9zYmkcE9Erp5gt0tigl0gWU6bPbzUruvvJ_MrCuHN3P4HjgmWSnl-qHzEj1okSc-m77UbEU_vTYLBqiLV4R-mHs4~ )
   Ledger Built a Wall Between AI Agents and Your Funds

Ledger released an open-source toolkit on July 16 that defines
exactly what AI
